subject: ControlLoop Models
commit_message:
ControlLoop Models

This commit introduces models directory which hosts model files used
by controlloop modules like runtime, participants etc

Issue-ID: POLICY-3215
Signed-off-by: Sirisha_Manchikanti <sirisha.manchikanti@est.tech>
Change-Id: Ie16f5e6a1004d88e996c321ed24d587818b8c770

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

we already ingested a large review.  Let's break this one into pieces.  Seems odd that there isn't a way to merge the two branches without re-adding all of the code

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> we already ingested a large review.  Let's break this one into pieces.  Seems odd that there isn't a way to merge the two branches without re-adding all of the code

Just on the merging,maybe we can cherry pick the participant code, but where we have code to be unified, it won't work. Let's think on it.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > we already ingested a large review.  Let's break this one into pieces.  Seems odd that there isn't a way to merge the two branches without re-adding all of the code
> 
> Just on the merging,maybe we can cherry pick the participant code, but where we have code to be unified, it won't work. Let's think on it.

I think it will be difficult to cherry pick from tosca-poc to master because the directory structure is different, but I'm not a git/gerrit expert. We of course can do a review that copies entire directories across but I guess that's what we're already doing.

Anyway, maybe it's not a bad idea to do that because it allows us to re-check everything before it goes to master. This of course gives extra work to reviewers and we really appreciate your help with this.

Patch Set 1:

> I think it will be difficult to cherry pick from tosca-poc to master because the directory structure is different, but I'm not a git/gerrit expert. We of course can do a review that copies entire directories across but I guess that's what we're already doing.
> 
> Anyway, maybe it's not a bad idea to do that because it allows us to re-check everything before it goes to master. This of course gives extra work to reviewers and we really appreciate your help with this.

OK, so could things just be moved in tosca-poc to where they're going to be in master?  Then the first review would simply be to approve the renaming.  Then a subsequent review could maybe do the merge.  (If the merge idea would work at all.)

Are all of these changes already merged under tosca-poc?  It kind of feels like they aren't.  For a bulk add like this, I just want it to be a simple clone of the code, with no associated changes (other than what is needed for the directory rename).

These files are merged under tosca-poc, in clamp/tosca-controlloop/models. I have copied models directory completely from tosca-poc (clamp/tosca-controlloop/models) to master (clamp/models). I will wait for your agreement before splitting this commit into pieces.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

> These files are merged under tosca-poc, in clamp/tosca-controlloop/models. I have copied models directory completely from tosca-poc (clamp/tosca-controlloop/models) to master (clamp/models). I will wait for your agreement before splitting this commit into pieces.

So this is just a straight copy?  Is this the last bulk add?  If the answer to both questions is, "yes", then we can leave it as is for the review.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: -Code-Review
> 
> > These files are merged under tosca-poc, in clamp/tosca-controlloop/models. I have copied models directory completely from tosca-poc (clamp/tosca-controlloop/models) to master (clamp/models). I will wait for your agreement before splitting this commit into pieces.
> 
> So this is just a straight copy?  Is this the last bulk add?  If the answer to both questions is, "yes", then we can leave it as is for the review.

Yes, This is a straight copy, with no changes except directory moved one level up from tosca-control-loop to clamp. 
This models were introduced in tosca-poc as part of below old commits
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/policy/clamp/+/117113
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/policy/clamp/+/118466
and they were evolved from then and had modifications in later commits including recent last huge commit https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/policy/clamp/+/121069

I am leaving this commit without splitting as agreed.
Uploaded patch set 2.

There is one other straight copy.

The participant code is a straight copy from the tosca-poc/tosca-controllooop/participant directory to the master/participant directory.

The CLAMP runtime code will not be a straight copy as we have to integrate the code from tosca-poc with the current CLAMP runtime.
